title: cerastium arvense var. molle
intro: Cerastium arvense var. molle, also known as a soft mouse-ear chickweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating skin irritations, wounds, and minor cuts. The health benefits of Cerastium arvense var. molle include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and soothing digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote skin health and alleviate digestive discomfort.
